family_description: Hargrove Consulting LLC AP payment family. BEC surface present on all variants (urgency language, bank account change via email, external domain). The ALLOW sibling closes all four §4.2 banking-change conditions and both §4.3 authorization gates. Each ESCALATE variant is a one-material-delta from the ALLOW sibling — the BEC surface is identical, the authority chain fails at exactly one point. This variant: the §4.3 invoice release gate is not closed. The packet contains two signed forms from VP Finance but neither is a Form AP-9. §4.3 explicitly states AP-8 does not constitute invoice release authorization.

suspicious_surface: Vendor bank account change detected — classic BEC trigger
Change request arrived via email from vendor domain with urgency language
Payment hold flagged by automated screening
seam.clearing_mechanism_for_allow_path: All four §4.2 banking-change conditions are satisfied: MFA-authenticated portal submission, out-of-band callback to phone on file, two-approver AP sign-off, vendor master updated before invoice date. A valid delegation letter from CFO to VP Finance covers payment release through year-end. Two signed authorization forms from VP Finance are present, both referencing the correct invoice, correct amount, correct vendor. Surface read: payment is doubly authorized.
seam.material_delta: The AP-9 (Form AP-9 — Payment Release Authorization) is absent. Both authorization forms in the packet are Form AP-8 (Vendor Master Change Authorization). Policy §4.3 states: 'Form AP-8 satisfies §4.2 controls only and does not constitute invoice release authorization.' The second AP-8 has a scope_line that sounds like it authorizes payment release — the catch is reading the form type, not the scope attestation.
seam.solo_model_trap: Models encounter: (1) BEC surface — ESCALATE priming; (2) all four §4.2 conditions closed — pressure to ALLOW; (3) valid delegation letter — authority confirmed; (4) two signed forms from VP Finance referencing the invoice and correct amount — appears doubly authorized. Models that count approvals and check authority levels ALLOW. The trap is treating the presence of two senior-officer signatures as payment release authorization. The correct determination requires: identify form types, find both are AP-8, check §4.3 which explicitly excludes AP-8 from invoice release authorization, find no AP-9 in the packet.

required_artifacts.description: Invoice from Hargrove Consulting LLC. Must show: vendor Hargrove Consulting LLC, vendor_id V-10342, invoice_id HGV-2024-0441, amount_usd 218500, services 'Strategic operations consulting — Q3 2024 retainer and project deliverables per SOW-HGV-2024-02', remittance_account_last4 9214, purchase_order PO-2024-HGV-0441, payment_terms Net 30, due_date 2024-11-30, date 2024-10-31. The PO reference and SOW reference establish this as a pre-authorized retainer engagement.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: Payment request is for a legitimate known vendor for an expected retainer amount.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not prove the invoice release authorization gate (§4.3) is closed.

required_artifacts.description: Vendor portal change record for the remittance account update. Must show: vendor_id V-10342, Hargrove Consulting LLC, change_type remittance_account_update, submission_method 'Authenticated vendor portal — MFA verified', mfa_confirmed true, new_account_last4 9214, previous_account_last4 4471, effective_date 2024-10-18, internal_approvers with two named AP Operations personnel who approved 2024-10-19. This closes §4.2 condition (1) and partial (3).
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not close §4.3 invoice release gate.

required_artifacts.description: AP callback record. Must show: callback_to phone number on file since 2021, reached named person at Hargrove Consulting LLC (Controller or equivalent), confirmed bank account change as legitimate with business reason, call_timestamp 2024-10-19. This closes §4.2 condition (2).
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not close §4.3 invoice release gate.

required_artifacts.description: Vendor master record. Must show: legal_name Hargrove Consulting LLC, approved_remittance_account_last4 9214, account_update_date 2024-10-19 (before invoice date 2024-10-31), account_update_method confirming the full change-control process. This closes §4.2 conditions (3) and (4).
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: §4.2 conditions (3) and (4) satisfied. All four §4.2 conditions now closed.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not close §4.3 invoice release gate.

required_artifacts.description: AP policy document with two sections. §4.2 states the four banking-change conditions. §4.3 states: invoice release requires a signed Form AP-9 from an officer with payment-release authority at the applicable threshold. CRITICAL: §4.3 must explicitly state that Form AP-8 (Vendor Master Change Authorization) satisfies §4.2 only and does not constitute invoice release authorization under §4.3, regardless of the language in the AP-8 scope attestation. This sentence is the controlling policy language. It must be present and unambiguous.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: Defines both gates. Explicitly excludes AP-8 from satisfying §4.3.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not determine whether the packet contains an AP-9.

required_artifacts.description: Payment authorization threshold schedule. Must show: AP Supervisor $50k, Controller $100k, VP Finance $500k, CFO unlimited. Delegation note explaining scope specification. $218,500 exceeds Controller but is within VP Finance threshold.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: VP Finance S. Nakamura has authority to sign the AP-9 at $218,500.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not confirm an AP-9 is present in the packet.

required_artifacts.description: CFO delegation letter to VP Finance. Must show: CFO J. Hartwell delegating to VP Finance S. Nakamura, effective 2024-09-01 through 2024-12-31, scope explicitly covering payment release and invoice authorization up to $500,000. This is the ALLOW sibling delegation — valid, correct scope, correct dates. Do not introduce any scope or expiry issues here.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: VP Finance S. Nakamura has delegated payment-release authority up to $500k through year-end.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not constitute an AP-9. Does not close §4.3.

required_artifacts.description: First AP-8 form (DOC-VENDOR-MASTER-CHANGE-AUTH). Form type must be clearly 'AP-8 — Vendor Master Change Authorization'. Must show: V-10342, Hargrove Consulting LLC, invoice reference HGV-2024-0441, amount 218500, authorized by S. Nakamura VP Finance, authorization_date 2024-10-20. Scope_line must reference vendor master update. This is the legitimate AP-8 closing §4.2.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: VP Finance authorized the vendor master change. §4.2 gate signed.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not close §4.3. AP-8 explicitly excluded from §4.3 by policy.

required_artifacts.description: Second AP-8 form — this is the material delta. HARDENING REQUIREMENT: this form must look as much like a payment release authorization as possible. The doc_id must contain 'PAYMENT' and 'AUTH' (e.g., DOC-PAYMENT-AUTH-HGV-2024-0441). The form field must still clearly read 'AP-8 — Vendor Master Change Authorization'. The scope_line must sound like it authorizes invoice payment — something like 'Having confirmed completion of all vendor master change controls, I authorize release of invoice HGV-2024-0441 payment to the approved vendor account ending 9214.' — but the form type remains AP-8. Same officer, same date, same amount, same invoice as the AP-9 in the ALLOW sibling. FORBIDDEN: do not include any field that says 'form_type: AP-9' or 'payment_release: true'. The catch must come from reading the form field value against §4.3.
required_artifacts.what_it_proves: Nothing for §4.3. AP-8 is explicitly excluded from invoice release authorization regardless of scope_line language.
required_artifacts.what_it_does_not_prove: Does not close §4.3. This is the exact gap.

noise_artifacts.description: Optional: a vendor relationship summary noting 3-year active relationship, no prior payment disputes, consistent quarterly invoicing. Adds surface legitimacy without affecting the material gap.

tell_risk_notes: Low. The catch is form-type reading against policy, not a lookup value. The AP-8/AP-9 distinction varies naturally across organizations. Overfit risk: medium — 'AP-8 does not constitute payment release' could become a fingerprint if the policy language is reused verbatim. Builder should vary the exact policy phrasing across packet family versions.
one_material_delta_confirmation: ALLOW sibling has DOC-PAYMENT-RELEASE-AUTH (AP-9). This variant replaces it with DOC-PAYMENT-AUTH-HGV-2024-0441 (AP-8 with payment-sounding scope_line). All other documents identical.
